title: Playground Keybar
concept: A tabbed, keyboard-like Playground workspace for first-time learners who want expert affordances: the live WordPress browser is the first viewport, global creation/saving/import/export actions live in compact structured menus, and Site Manager tools are split into fast tabs rather than modal panels.
focus: tabbed workspace with live browser first
structured command menus instead of explanatory onboarding
high-contrast black, white, and cyan technical palette
compact keyboard-like affordances for learner discovery
flows: Navigate and refresh the active WordPress site from the first viewport while opening Homepage and WP Admin from the preview toolbar.

Start a Playground from Vanilla WordPress, WordPress PR, Gutenberg PR or branch, GitHub import, Blueprint URL, imported zip, featured Blueprint shortcuts, or the full Blueprint gallery through New and Import menus.
Save a temporary Playground in browser storage or to a local directory with visible file-copy progress, then manage saved and unsaved entries with rename and delete actions.
Use Site Manager tabs for runtime settings, file browser, Blueprint editing, database tools, logs, export to GitHub, and zip download.
Browse and filter all 43 Blueprint gallery entries while keeping current Blueprint copy, download, and run commands available from the toolbar.
featureCoverage: create from Gutenberg PR or branch
import from GitHub after account connection
browse and filter Blueprint gallery entries
save temporary Playground in browser storage
navigate active WordPress paths with URL input
refresh the active embedded WordPress page
understand destructive reset and stored Save & Reload behavior
use File browser with create folder/file, upload, browse, and code editing affordances
